findings.description: Two-column license code/tax listing in the PDF is flattened into a single wide table in the HTML, changing the page’s visual structure.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the code/license/tax list is presented as two side-by-side grouped columns, each with its own Code Number / License Description / Tax headers. In the HTML, this content appears as one continuous six-column table spanning the page.

findings.description: Table data integrity issue: the tax value for code 451 (Real Estate) is missing in the HTML table.
findings.evidence: PDF table shows code 451 'Real Estate (Only one license required if engaged in multiple real estate activities)' with tax '$50.00'. HTML splits the description across multiple rows and leaves the Tax cells blank for code 451.

findings.description: Text accuracy issue in the table: omission of the $50.00 tax for the Real Estate entry changes the meaning of the licensing information.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the right-hand table lists '451 Real Estate ... $50.00'. In the HTML, the corresponding rows for '451' and its continuation lines contain no tax amount.
